title: Ctenitis abundans
taxonomy: Ctenitis abundans is classified under the family Dryopteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or male ferns. It belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of epiphytic ferns native to tropical regions. The genus Ctenitis is further categorized under the class Polypodiopsida and division Pteridophyta. This classification places Ctenitis abundans within the broader plant kingdom, highlighting its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The specific classification of Ctenitis abundans reflects its distinct morphological features and ecological adaptations.
uses: Ctenitis abundans is reportedly used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Traditionally, it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, which have been the subject of scientific research. Further studies are needed to fully understand and validate the medicinal uses of Ctenitis abundans.

intro: Ctenitis abundans, also known as the abundant tree fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, improve wound healing, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis abundans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Ctenitis abundans is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly in traditional medicine practices in Asia.
